_note: The upstream codec YAML ships no examples, so vectors are synthetic. Inputs are hand-built real-time frames (function_code 0x41, data_type 0x01) with all decoded values held inside vocabulary bounds. expected.data was produced by the ORACLE method: the upstream decoder (reference/upstream-codec.js) is run with its only transcription bug fixed (its `Decoder` body reads an undefined `data` identifier instead of the `bytes` argument; run as-is it throws ReferenceError), then the resulting fields are normalized per codec.js. The T&H model has no CO2 sensor, so the shared decoder's co2 field is always 0 on the wire.

uplink.description: Nominal real-time frame: T=23.5C, RH=52.3%, battery=85%
Negative temperature: T=-10.0C, RH=10.0%, battery=100% (exercises sub-baseline temp math)
High humidity edge: T=30.0C, RH=99.9%, battery=20% (exercises 12-bit humidity nibble math)
Unsupported frame (function_code 0x42, not real-time) returns an error
Truncated real-time frame (header only) returns an error
